projects.title: WeRateDogs
description: I started working on "We Rate Dogs" with Brian back in 2022. This is a key example of how simplicity is not a synonym of bad strategy in ecom: with an average of 200k monthly visitors, it's clear that social media influence + good products are the heart of ecom, and the website is just the door. A beautiful door doesn't always lead to a beautiful house.

description.alt: SimilarWeb traffic stats for weratedogs.com showing roughly 200k monthly visitors

list: Full redesign of the mobile and desktop product page: custom slider, swatch-based variant selection, click-to-zoom, stacked desktop images
Alternate template system so single-variant products skip the variant dropdown automatically, with per-product layout switching for merchants
Metafield-driven related products, layered on top of Shopify's native recommendation engine
Dynamic price updates on variant change, sold-out state handling, and cross-device QA (iOS version-specific bugs, older phone rendering issues)
Klaviyo back-in-stock notification system: metafield toggle per product, list/flow wiring, variant-level subscriber tracking
Klaviyo signup form styling fixes (custom CSS overrides for popup and full-page forms)
Wistia and YouTube video embeds, including a fix for a hardcoded video ID bug and an iOS-specific playback truncation issue
Diagnosed and resolved a third-party app (Spurit) injecting code that delayed the Add to Cart button's render
Site speed and rendering fixes (image slider height inconsistencies, font sizing, layout bugs across launches)
Blog page cleanup and mobile embed styling (TikTok embeds)
Theme architecture consulting for a planned site redesign (Dawn vs. Impulse theme recommendation)
Extended trust to manage sister site Thoughts of Dog and referred additional client work

projects.title: Zudo
description: I, along with Zudo's owner, Ibraheem Nadeem, have collaborated closely since 2018 to elevate and refine his e-commerce platform.

Our project revolves around a customized theme built upon Archetype's Impulse theme, with a significant emphasis on Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) and A/B testing. To facilitate this, we've developed specialized sections tailored explicitly for A/B testing purposes.
Recently, we finalized the implementation of a custom bundle page aimed at enhancing client engagement and increasing average customer spending.

projects.title: Sharicca
description: At the request of the Sharicca owner, I coded the entire website from the ground up. The most complex feature was the mega-menu/navigation and hero section, which I rebuilt entirely from the ground up.
For the hero section, we integrated a dynamic Flickity slideshow to enhance visual engagement. The mega-menu was meticulously coded using only HTML/CSS3, without relying on additional libraries.
By leveraging hover animations, we eliminated the need for JavaScript, ensuring performance and seamless functionality across all devices, including mobile and tablets.
The mega-menu is also wired up to the customize page and navigation items, so the merchant can easily update all of its text, colors, links and icons.

projects.title: Light My Bricks
description: Light My Bricks is a globally recognized Australian company specializing in LED for LEGO. This theme was meticulously engineered to deliver optimal performance and a top-notch user experience. To achieve this, we abstained from using jQuery and minimized reliance on third-party libraries, except for essential ones such as Flickity for enhanced UX in sliders.
On the backend, merchants benefit from easy and accessible customization options via the Customize page and metafields.
